hail , unvexed with care and strife , The bliss of Academic Life ; Where kind repose protracts the span , While Childhood ripens into man ; Where no hard parent's dreaded rage Kerbs the gay sports of youthful age ; Where no vile fear the Genius awes With grim severity of laws ; Where annual troops of Bucks come down , The flower of every neighbouring town ; Where wealth and pride and riot wait , And every rogue may find his mate

( While various thus their doom appears , These lose their souls , and those their ears ) The want of fancy , whim supplies , And native humour

( While various thus their doom appears , These lose their souls , and those their ears ) The want of fancy , whim supplies , And native humour , mad caprice ; Loud noise for argument goes off , For mirth polite , the ribald's scoff ; For sense , lewd droll'ries
